title: Add Self-Contained Spec CLI Unit Tests
description: Create and run the standalone unittest suite for the spec-management CLI, covering initialization, creation, discovery, listing, showing, setters, migration, legacy-spec handling, and clean error paths.
acceptance_criteria: `skills/spec-management/tests/test_spec.py` covers the subcommands and error cases enumerated in the spec.
Tests verify both normal frontmatter specs and legacy specs without frontmatter.
Tests assert specified user-facing error and warning messages, including ambiguous and missing lookup behavior.
The tester notes that `python3 -m unittest discover -s skills/spec-management/tests/` is the authoritative CLI test run and that the orchestrator suite is only a regression check.

handoff_summary.completed: Created `skills/spec-management/tests/__init__.py` and `skills/spec-management/tests/test_spec.py` with 82 tests across 17 test classes. Tests cover: `_split_frontmatter` (4 cases), `parse_frontmatter` (3), `is_legacy_spec` (3), `infer_display_name` (3), `_title_to_filename` (4), `write_frontmatter` (2), `find_all_specs` (3), `resolve_spec` (6 — including no-match, ambiguous, exact-ID-wins), `cmd_init` (3), `cmd_create` (5), `cmd_list` (7 — filters + legacy + headers), `cmd_show` (3 — normal, legacy warning, missing), `cmd_set status` (4), `cmd_set other fields` (6 — feature-root, tags, priority, description, unknown field, legacy hint), `cmd_migrate` (6 — migrate, fields, heading inference, prints ID, already-has-frontmatter, infers status from dir), `_status_from_path` (4), `_normalize_tags` (4), guards (4), `build_parser` (8). Run: `python3 -m unittest discover -s skills/spec-management/tests/ -v` → 82 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. All acceptance criteria are met: subcommands and error cases covered, both normal and legacy specs tested, user-facing error/warning messages asserted (ambiguous lookup, missing lookup, migrate hint on set), and the authoritative test run is documented in the test module docstring.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Only new files added under skills/spec-management/tests/; no src/ or existing files modified.
handoff_summary.next_action: No follow-up needed. Bead is complete.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only new files added under skills/spec-management/tests/. No src/ files touched. No overlap with any other in-progress bead file-scope claims.

execution_history.summary: Created `skills/spec-management/tests/test_spec.py` with 82 unit tests covering all spec-management CLI subcommands (init, create, list, show, set, migrate), frontmatter utilities, spec discovery, ID resolution, guards, and error paths. All 82 tests pass. Also created `skills/spec-management/tests/__init__.py`. The authoritative test run is `python3 -m unittest discover -s skills/spec-management/tests/`; the orchestrator suite (uv run python -m unittest discover -s tests -v) is unaffected as no src/ files were touched.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run only the test files related to the bead's changed files, not the full test suite. Use `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v` to target individual test files rather than `discover`. If unsure which tests are relevant, use the bead's expected_files and touched_files fields as a guide.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes (e.g. import corrections, fixture setup) only when strictly necessary to run the relevant tests. Do not refactor production code under the guise of test enablement.

Disallowed actions:
- Run the full test suite with `discover`. Always target the specific module: `uv run python -m unittest tests.<module_name> -v`. Running `discover` wastes time and often exceeds the agent timeout.
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Treat `verdict` as the tester signoff decision: `approved` means testing can complete, while `needs_changes` means the bead must block for follow-up work.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when testing is complete with no unresolved tester-scope findings.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the unresolved defect or coverage gap count, set `requires_followup=true` unless there is a stronger explicit reason not to, and always include `block_reason` when follow-up work is required.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as free-form narrative context only. They inform operators, but they do not override the structured verdict or control scheduler state.
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
